skill.name: notebooklm-slides
skill.description: Generate NotebookLM slide deck prompts for educational chapters, calibrated by learner proficiency and instructional goals.
skill.summary: Create proficiency-calibrated NotebookLM slide deck workflows for educational content.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found a Markdown workflow, not executable source code. The shell examples are limited to starting a local browser helper and moving a downloaded PDF, while the cryptography alerts are false positives from normal Markdown text.

low_findings.title: Local shell commands in Markdown workflow
low_findings.description: The skill documents shell usage to start a local browser helper and move a generated PDF. The commands are transparent and tied to the stated slide workflow, but users should review paths before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains explicit shell command examples, but they are fixed workflow commands and do not show user-input command construction or hidden execution.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations do not contain cryptographic algorithm use, hashing APIs, encryption code, or password handling. They are normal Markdown metadata, prose, tables, and prompt examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic primitives or security-sensitive cryptographic behavior at the reported locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: External command examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The Markdown includes a bash quick-start command and an mv example. These are not malicious, but they require local shell execution if a user chooses to follow them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and fixed, so the execution pattern is real while the evidence for malicious behavior is low.

content.value_statement: Educational slide decks often become generic when prompts lack audience and learning context. This skill provides NotebookLM workflows and prompt structures for proficiency-calibrated chapter presentations.
actual_capabilities: Defines a step-by-step NotebookLM workflow for creating chapter slide decks.
Provides prompt templates for A2, B1, and C1 learner proficiency levels.
Lists seven review gates for checking slide quality before deployment.
Specifies PDF naming and README frontmatter integration guidance.
Includes batch processing advice for generating slides across multiple chapters.
limitations: Requires NotebookLM and a browser automation workflow; it does not generate slides directly.
Depends on NotebookLM availability, generation time, and daily usage limits.
Focuses on educational chapter decks, not sales, marketing, or general presentations.
Requires source material quality review before upload to NotebookLM.

use_cases.title: Prepare beginner course slides
use_cases.description: Convert a chapter and quiz materials into a NotebookLM prompt for A2 learners with simple language and action-oriented endings.

use_cases.title: Standardize instructor decks
use_cases.description: Use the review gates and naming rules to keep slide decks consistent across course chapters.

use_cases.title: Create advanced technical presentations
use_cases.description: Adapt the C1 prompt structure for expert learners who need trade-offs, decision matrices, and deployment strategy slides.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Chapter Deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a first-pass slide deck for new learners.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the notebooklm-slides workflow for an A2 audience. Build a chapter deck from these sources, emphasize simple explanations, and apply the seven review gates.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate Course Deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn practical course material into a structured presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a B1 NotebookLM slide prompt for this chapter. Include prerequisites, practical examples, clear themes, and implementation steps at the end.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Practitioner Deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare a rigorous deck for experienced learners.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a C1 NotebookLM slide prompt for advanced practitioners. Include trade-offs, industry patterns, decision matrices, and production deployment strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Slide Production
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate multiple chapter decks with consistent structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a batch workflow for three or more NotebookLM chapter decks. Include source upload order, prompt preparation, PDF naming, and quality checks.

output_examples.input: A chapter about AI coding tools for learners with no programming experience.
output_examples.output: An A2 NotebookLM prompt that asks for 12 to 15 slides, simple language, concrete examples, and specific next steps.

output_examples.input: A technical chapter for experienced engineers about production deployment.
output_examples.output: A C1 slide prompt with 20 to 25 slides, decision matrices, architecture diagrams, trade-offs, and deployment strategies.

output_examples.input: A request to publish generated slides inside a course site.
output_examples.output: A naming and integration checklist covering PDF placement, chapter frontmatter, and visual review before deployment.

best_practices: Upload all chapter sources before generating the NotebookLM slide deck.
State audience level, themes, tone, and slide count in every prompt.
Review generated slides against all seven success gates before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague audience labels when learner proficiency matters.
Do not rely on NotebookLM default formatting for educational decks.
Do not publish slides with generic endings or missing chapter themes.

faq.question: Does this skill create the slide files directly?
faq.answer: No. It guides NotebookLM prompt creation, review, download, naming, and integration.

faq.question: Which learner levels are covered?
faq.answer: The workflow includes A2 beginner, B1 intermediate, and C1 advanced prompt structures.

faq.question: Can I use it for non-educational decks?
faq.answer: It is designed for educational chapter presentations. General business decks may need different prompts.

faq.question: Does it require browser automation?
faq.answer: The documented workflow expects NotebookLM access through a browser and references a browser-use helper.

faq.question: How are generated PDFs named?
faq.answer: The skill recommends zero-padded chapter names such as chapter-01-slides.pdf.

faq.question: How should I judge slide quality?
faq.answer: Use the seven gates for title, language, themes, tone, count, narrative arc, and ending.
